risk.desc: Potential structural weakness in the cutaway window area due to reduced material.
risk.mit: Reinforce the cutaway window area with additional material or support structures.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to lack of precise alignment features.
risk.mit: Add alignment pins or keys to ensure proper mating between components.

risk.desc: Engine display cartridge may not be fully removable without disassembling the entire module.
risk.mit: Ensure that the engine display cartridge has clear access points and removal features.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion causing issues in the bolt circle dummy connections.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion compensators or flexible joints in the bolt circle dummy connections.

risk.desc: Potential for strain on cables exiting the cutaway window without proper strain relief.
risk.mit: Implement strain relief features for all cable exits from the cutaway window.

Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
Tank, interstage, avionics, recovery, and engine-display modules are labeled educational/display-only.
